title: DNA extraction and Nested PCR
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"661gp","text":"This is the protocol for the Nested PCR assay","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"chcvq","text":"All primer sequences are listed below:","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bjt3d","text":"The first round of PCR was carried out using EbGeno-fe (5′-TTC AGA TGG TCA TAG GGA TG-3′) as the forward primer and EbGeno-re (5′-ATT AGA GCA TTC CGT GAGG-3′) as the reverse primer,whichtogether amplify a 465-bp specific fragment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"j43u","text":"A second round of PCR was performed using the forward primer EbGeno-fi (5′-TCG GCT CTG AAT ATC TAT GG-3′) and the reverse primer EbGeno-ri (5′-ATT CTT TCG CGC TCG TC-3′) in order to amplify a 410-bp internal fragment used for genotype specification.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"8t3l8","text":"DNase/RNase-Free Deionized Water,  primer, TaKaRa Taq DNA Polymerase (TaKaRa Bio Inc., Tokyo, Japan), 10×PCR Buffer, 10 mmol dNTP, 1.5 % agarose gel, GelStrain","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: PCR amplification Amplification was accomplished in final volume of 25 μL, containing 17 μL of DNase/RNase-Free Deionized Water, 0.5 μL of each primer, 0.5 μL of TaKaRa Taq DNA Polymerase (TaKaRa Bio Inc., Tokyo, Japan), 2.5 μL of 10 × PCR Buffer, 2 μL of 10 mmol dNTP and 2 μL of DNA .
The target DNA undergoes the first run of PCR with the first set of primers. 00:05:00 Initial denaturation 95 °C

DNA extraction Genomic DNA of E. bieneusi was extracted directly from 180 to 200 mg of each fecal specimen using the recommended procedures and the provided reagents by the manufacture of QIAamp DNA Stool Mini Kit (QIAGEN, Hilden, Germany). To obtain high yield of DNA, the lysis temperature was increased to 95 ° C according to the manufacturer’s suggestion. DNA was eluted in 200 μL of AE and stored at −20°C before it was used for PCR analysis.
The product from the first reaction undergoes a second run of PCR with the second set of primers. 00:05:00 Initial denaturation 95 °C
Final hold: The final step cools the reaction chamber to 4–15 °C (39–59 °F) for an indefinite time, and may be employed for short-term storage of the PCR products.
All secondary PCR products were subjected to electrophoresis in a 1.5 % agarose gel and visualized by staining the gel with GelStrain (TransGen Biotech., Beijing, China).
Final hold The final step cools the reaction chamber to 4°C for an indefinite time, and may be employed for short-term storage of the PCR products.
All secondary PCR products were subjected to electrophoresis in a 1.5 % agarose gel and visualized by staining the gel with GelStrain (TransGen Biotech., Beijing, China).
creator: Baiyan Gong
